description: In shell, use the package-local commands (the script under its declared shell (#!/usr/bin/env bash vs POSIX sh)) rather than a root or generic command.
body: In shell, the real contract lives in the owning package's config; a root or generic command can use the wrong the target shell and the coreutils flavor (GNU vs BSD vs busybox) or skip checks. Use when building, testing, or analyzing a specific shell package, especially in a monorepo. Do not assume a top-level command covers the package, and avoid running a generic command that ignores its config. Actions: find the package's own targets, run the script under its declared shell (#!/usr/bin/env bash vs POSIX sh) and bats tests or a guarded dry-run with set -euo pipefail scoped to it, and compare results against the package's configured gate. Validation signals include the package-scoped command log, its exit code, and a shellcheck warnings (especially SC2086 word-splitting and SC2046) pass using the package config. Risk boundary: escalate if no package-local command exists or if it depends on environment you cannot safely provision.
